title: 11 Herbal Creams For Fever Blister On Lip
intro_desc: Herbal creams can be a great help in soothing fever blisters on your lip. These creams typically contain natural ingredients like Aloe barbadensis, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can reduce swelling and ease pain. Calendula officinalis is another herb used in these creams, known for its ability to promote healing and reduce redness. Echinacea purpurea, a popular herb for immune system support, is also sometimes added to help prevent future outbreaks. When applied topically, these herbal ingredients can calm the affected area and promote a speedy recovery.  But how do these herbal creams work? Well, Aloe barbadensis has a cooling effect that can help reduce the itching and burning sensation associated with fever blisters. This can make it more comfortable to eat and speak without worrying about discomfort. Calendula officinalis, on the other hand, has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent infection and promote wound healing. And Echinacea purpurea can help boost your immune system, reducing the likelihood of future outbreaks.  Using herbal creams for fever blisters on your lip can bring several benefits to your life. For one, they can help you avoid the pain and discomfort associated with these blisters. They can also help you save time and money by reducing the number of doctor visits and medication prescriptions. And, by promoting a speedy recovery, they can get you back to your normal activities sooner. Plus, many herbal creams are gentle and non-irritating, making them suitable for sensitive skin.
intro_image_alt: herbal creams for fever blister on lip

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe barbadensis creams contains several bioactive constituents, including aloin, aloe-emodin, and vitamins A, C, and E. These compounds have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and soothing properties that help to reduce redness and swelling associated with fever blisters on the lip. The aloe-emodin in Aloe barbadensis creams has been shown to inhibit the herpes simplex virus, the primary cause of fever blisters. Additionally, the vitamins in Aloe barbadensis creams, particularly vitamin A, help to promote healing and reduce the risk of scarring. By applying Aloe barbadensis creams to the affected area, you can take advantage of its natural properties to help alleviate the symptoms of fever blisters on the lip.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe barbadensis herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup Aloe barbadensis gel, 1/4 cup coconut oil, 2 tablespoons beeswax, 2 tablespoons vitamin E oil, and 10 drops of tea tree oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Once melted, remove from heat and stir in Aloe barbadensis gel, vitamin E oil, and tea tree oil until well combined.
Pour the mixture into a small container or tin and let it cool and solidify at room temperature.
Once solid, store the Aloe barbadensis cream in the fridge and apply a small amount to the affected lip area as needed to soothe fever blisters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis creams contains triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and carotenoids as its active constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce redness and swelling associated with fever blisters on the lip. The flavonoids present in Calendula officinalis creams also have antimicrobial properties, which inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses that can cause fever blisters. The anti-inflammatory properties of triterpenoid saponins help to soothe and calm the skin, reducing the pain and discomfort associated with fever blisters. By applying Calendula officinalis creams to the affected area, you can help to speed up the healing process and reduce the severity of fever blisters on the lip.
remedies.image_alt: Calendula officinalis herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of Calendula officinalis petals, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Infuse Calendula petals in coconut oil by mixing them in a bowl and letting it sit in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the bowl every day.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a new bowl, squeezing the petals to extract as much oil as possible.
In a double boiler, melt the beeswax and shea butter over low heat. Add the strained Calendula oil and vitamin E oil. Stir until well combined.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the mixture cool and thicken. Pour the mixture into a jar and let it cool completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea creams contains active constituents like alkylamides, glycoproteins, and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties. These properties help to reduce the severity and duration of fever blisters on the lip by soothing the skin and preventing the spread of the herpes simplex virus. The glycoproteins in Echinacea purpurea creams stimulate the immune system, which helps to fight off the virus and speed up the healing process. The phenolic acids also have antimicrobial properties, which help to prevent infection and promote a healthy environment for healing. By applying Echinacea purpurea creams to fever blisters, you can reduce pain, swelling, and the risk of scarring.
remedies.image_alt: Echinacea purpurea herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 tablespoons of dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil, and 2 drops of tea tree oil.
Infuse the Echinacea flowers in 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut oil) for 2-3 weeks, shaking the mixture daily.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Strain the infused oil and add it to the melted beeswax and coconut oil mixture, then stir in vitamin E oil and tea tree oil.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let it cool and harden before applying to fever blisters as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia creams contains high levels of cineole, a compound known for its antiviral properties. This makes it effective against the herpes simplex virus, the cause of fever blisters on the lip. The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of cineole help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with fever blisters. The tea tree oil in Melaleuca alternifolia creams also has antimicrobial properties, which prevent bacterial infections that can further irritate the affected area. The combination of these properties makes Melaleuca alternifolia creams a potential treatment option for fever blisters on the lip.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, 2 tablespoons of olive oil, 10 drops of tea tree oil, and 5 drops of lavender oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add shea butter and olive oil to the melted mixture. Stir until combined.
Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Stir in tea tree oil and lavender oil.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar. Let cool and harden before use. Apply a small amount to fever blisters as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams contain active constituents like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These compounds help reduce swelling and pain associated with fever blisters on the lip. Gingerol also has antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent infection and promote healing of the blister. The creams also contain antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which can help soothe and calm the skin. The anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties of Zingiber officinale creams make them a useful treatment for fever blisters on the lip.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of aloe vera gel, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of shea butter, and 10 drops of ginger essential oil.
Melt the coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in the microwave (10-second intervals, stirring between each interval).
In a separate bowl, mix the aloe vera gel and shea butter until well combined.
Add the ginger essential oil to the aloe vera gel mixture and stir well.
Slowly pour the melted coconut oil mixture into the aloe vera gel mixture and stir until well combined. Pour into a container and let cool before using.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams contains glycyrrhizin, a triterpenoid saponin that has anti-inflammatory properties. Glycyrrhizin reduces redness and swelling in fever blisters, making it easier to heal. The cream also contains flavonoids, like licoric acid, which have antiviral properties that help fight the herpes simplex virus that causes fever blisters. Glycyrrhizin and flavonoids in the cream help soothe and calm the skin, reducing pain and discomfort. By reducing inflammation and fighting the virus, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams can help speed up the healing process of fever blisters on the lip.
remedies.image_alt: Glycyrrhiza glabra herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of distilled water, 1/4 cup of glycerin, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of coconut oil, 2 teaspoons of licorice extract (Glycyrrhiza glabra), and a few drops of vitamin E oil.
Melt beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
In a separate bowl, mix distilled water and glycerin. Add licorice extract and vitamin E oil. Stir well.
Slowly add the water-glycerin mixture to the melted beeswax and coconut oil. Stir until well combined.
Pour the mixture into small containers. Let it cool and harden. Apply the cream to fever blisters on the lip as needed.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus creams contains cineole, a key active constituent that helps soothe fever blisters on the lip. Cineole has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain in the affected area. The menthol in Eucalyptus globulus creams also helps to cool and calm the skin, making it less irritated and tender. Eucalyptus globulus creams also contain flavonoids and tannins, which have antiseptic properties that help prevent infection and promote healing. By applying Eucalyptus globulus creams to fever blisters, you can reduce discomfort and promote the healing process.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 2 cups of coconut oil, 1 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus essential oil, and a small bowl.
Melt the coconut oil in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl in the microwave (30 seconds at a time, stirring between each heating).
Add 1 cup of beeswax to the melted coconut oil and stir until the beeswax is fully dissolved.
Remove the mixture from the heat and add 2 tablespoons of eucalyptus essential oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into a small tin or jar and let it cool and harden. Your Eucalyptus globulus cream is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum creams contains hyperforin, a bioactive compound that has anti-inflammatory properties. These properties can help reduce swelling and redness associated with fever blisters on the lip. The cream also contains flavonoids, including rutin and quercetin, which have antiviral properties that can help fight the herpes simplex virus, the cause of fever blisters. Hypericum perforatum's antiviral and anti-inflammatory properties may contribute to its ability to shorten the duration of fever blisters and reduce their severity. The cream's soothing and protective effects can also help prevent the formation of scabs and promote wound healing on the affected area.
remedies.image_alt: Hypericum perforatum herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of beeswax, 1/2 cup of coconut oil, 1/4 cup of shea butter, and 2 tablespoons of Hypericum perforatum oil.
Melt the beeswax, coconut oil, and shea butter in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of boiling water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool for 5 minutes.
Add 2 tablespoons of Hypericum perforatum oil to the mixture and stir well.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers and let it cool and solidify before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Arnica montana creams contains a high concentration of sesquiterpene lactones, particularly thymoquinone and thymohydroquinone, which have anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help reduce swelling and redness associated with fever blisters on the lip. Arnica montana also contains flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have antiviral properties, potentially helping to combat the herpes simplex virus that causes fever blisters. The anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties of Arnica montana work together to soothe and calm the affected area, promoting healing and reducing the risk of scarring. By applying Arnica montana creams to fever blisters on the lip, you can potentially reduce the severity and duration of the outbreak.
remedies.image_alt: Arnica montana herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of carrier oil (coconut or olive oil), 2 tablespoons of Arnica montana oil, 2 tablespoons of beeswax, and 10 drops of tea tree essential oil.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Add the carrier oil and Arnica montana oil to the melted beeswax and stir until combined.
Remove the mixture from heat and add the tea tree essential oil. Stir well and pour the mixture into a heat-proof container.
Let the mixture cool and solidify, then apply a small amount to the affected lip area 2-3 times a day to help soothe fever blisters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Aloe vera creams contains several bioactive constituents, including aloe-emodin, aloin, and vitamins A, C, and E. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and soothing properties that can help reduce the pain and swelling associated with fever blisters on the lips. The antibacterial properties of aloe-emodin and aloin can also help combat infections that can cause fever blisters to become more painful and long-lasting. The vitamins A and E in aloe vera creams have antioxidant properties that can help protect the skin from further irritation and promote healing. The anti-inflammatory properties of the compounds in aloe vera creams can also help reduce redness and promote a faster recovery from fever blisters.
remedies.image_alt: Aloe vera herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Aloe vera gel and 1/4 cup of coconut oil.
In a small bowl, mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax and 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a double boiler or in a microwave-safe bowl.
Melt the beeswax and coconut oil mixture until smooth. Let it cool slightly.
Add 1/4 cup of melted beeswax mixture to the Aloe vera gel and mix well. Add 10 drops of tea tree oil for its antiviral properties.
Pour the mixture into a small container and let it cool and solidify before using as a lip balm for fever blisters.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita creams contains menthol, menthone, and limonene as its primary bioactive constituents. Menthol has a cooling effect that helps reduce pain and inflammation caused by fever blisters on the lip. Menthone and limonene have antibacterial and antiviral properties that help combat the herpes simplex virus responsible for causing fever blisters. The antiseptic properties of these compounds also help prevent the spread of infection and promote healing. By reducing pain and inflammation, and fighting the underlying infection, Mentha x piperita creams can help alleviate symptoms and promote recovery from fever blisters on the lip.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal creams for fever blister on lip
remedy_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of coconut oil, 1/2 cup of beeswax, 2 tablespoons of mentha x piperita essential oil, and 2 tablespoons of vitamin E oil.
Melt coconut oil and beeswax in a double boiler or a heat-proof bowl set over a pot of simmering water.
Remove the melted mixture from heat and let it cool slightly. Add mentha x piperita essential oil and vitamin E oil. Stir well to combine.
Pour the mixture into small tin containers or lip balm tubes. Let it cool and harden completely.
Once hardened, label and store the Mentha x piperita creams in a cool, dry place. Apply as needed to soothe fever blisters on the lip.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent fever blisters from forming. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe the skin and reduce the risk of an outbreak. Drinking these teas regularly may help keep your skin healthy and reduce the appearance of fever blisters.
faq_safe: You can drink herbal teas daily for fever blisters on your lip, but be careful not to overdo it. Some teas, like peppermint and chamomile, can help soothe and calm your skin. However, drinking too much can cause stomach issues. Limit your tea consumption to a few cups a day to avoid any problems.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with fever blisters on the lip. Some people start to feel relief within 24 to 48 hours after drinking these teas regularly. The herbs in these teas, such as peppermint and chamomile, can help reduce the pain and swelling of the fever blister. They can also help soothe the skin.
faq_time: When you have a fever blister on your lip, drinking herbal tea can be soothing. It's a good idea to drink it in the morning, as the caffeine can help reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The tea can also help you feel more alert and awake to tackle the day.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with prescription medications for fever blisters. Some herbal teas, like St. John's Wort, can affect how your body processes certain medications. If you're taking prescription medications for fever blisters, be cautious when drinking herbal teas like echinacea, sage, or peppermint, as they may increase the risk of side effects or reduce the medication's effectiveness.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas. Some teas, like echinacea and sage, may affect the baby. Fever blisters on the lip are usually caused by a virus, and herbal teas won't cure them. If you want to try a tea, peppermint might be safe, but only in small amounts and for a short time.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas can be a soothing option for fever blisters on your lip, but when breastfeeding, it's best to be cautious. Some herbal teas, like echinacea and sage, have been known to affect milk supply. It's a good idea to check the ingredients and choose teas that are safe for breastfeeding moms.
title_2: 11 Soothing Herbal Creams for Nursing Mothers with Fever Blister on Lip
